steps.title: Find the slope using the two points
steps.content:
From the table, we have the points $(-18, -48)$ and $(7, 52)$.
Slope: $m = \frac{52 - (-48)}{7 - (-18)} = \frac{100}{25} = 4$

steps.title: Write the linear equation using point-slope form
steps.content:
Using the point $(7, 52)$:
$$y - 52 = 4(x - 7)$$
$$y = 4x - 28 + 52$$
$$y = 4x + 24$$
We can verify with the other point: $y = 4(-18) + 24 = -72 + 24 = -48$ ✓
